en.extract:
The blue-fronted redstart (Phoenicurus frontalis) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae, the Old World flycatchers. It breeds in central China and the Himalayas (where it winters in the southern foothills, as well as in southern Yunnan, the Patkai and northern Indochina. Its natural habitat is temperate forests.  The female is brownish-grey, with paler underparts.


Gallery

The '''blue-fronted redstart''' ('''''Phoenicurus frontalis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Old World flycatcher|Muscicapidae]], the Old World flycatchers. It breeds in central China and the [[Himalayas]] (where it winters in the southern foothills, as well as in southern [[Yunnan]], the [[Patkai]] and northern [[Indochina]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is temperate [[forest]]s.  The female is brownish-grey, with paler underparts.

zh.extract:
蓝额红尾鸲（学名：Phoenicurus frontalis），是雀形目鹟科的一种鸟类。


特征
蓝额红尾鸲体重约15.77克，翼长约84.5毫米，嘴峰长约13.1毫米，喙宽度约3.1毫米，喙厚度约3.2毫米，跗蹠长约22.1毫米，尾长约69毫米。蓝额红尾鸲是部分迁徙的鸟类，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


分布
南亚的喜马拉雅山；越冬于东南亚越冬。
